debug_info.goal:
case succ
α✝ : Type
hα : OracleComp.SampleableType α✝
α : Type
inst✝ : OracleComp.SampleableType α
m : ℕ
ih :
  ∀ (x : Vector α m),
    x ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) m)
x : Vector α (m + 1)
⊢ x ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1))

failed_attempts:
simp [ih, Vector.support]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Vector.get, Option.getE, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero])
    <;>
    (try dsimp)
    <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        use x.pop, x.back
        <;>
        simp_all [Vector.get, Option.getE, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero]
        <;>
        aesop
      })

-- Inductive step: assume the statement holds for m, prove for m + 1
      -- We need to show that any vector of length m + 1 is in the support.
      -- A vector of length m + 1 can be written as push v a, where v is of length m and a is of type α.
      -- By the induction hypothesis, v is in the support of the sample for vectors of length m.
      -- Also, a is in the support of the sample for α.
      -- Therefore, push v a is in the support of the sample for vectors of length m + 1.
      have h₁ : ∃ (v : Vector α m) (a : α), x = Vector.push v a := by
        have h₂ : x.length = m + 1 := by
          simp_all [Vector.length]
          <;> omega
        have h₃ : x.length = m + 1 := by simpa using h₂
        have h₄ : ∃ (v : Vector α m) (a : α), x = Vector.push v a := by
          have h₅ : x.length = m + 1 := by simpa using h₃
          have h₆ : ∃ (v : Vector α m) (a : α), x = Vector.push v a := by
            cases x using Vector.recOn with
            | nil => simp_all [Vector.length]
            | cons x xs =>
              -- Case: x = cons x xs
              -- We need to find v and a such that cons x xs = push v a
              -- We can set v = xs and a = x, but we need to check the lengths
              -- xs has length m, and cons x xs has length m + 1, so this works
              have h₇ : xs.length = m := by
                simp_all [Vector.length]
                <;> omega
              have h₈ : xs.length = m := by simpa using h₇
              refine' ⟨xs, x, _⟩
              <;> simp_all [Vector.push, Vector.cons_val_zero, Vector.cons_val_succ, Vector.head_cons]
              <;>
              (try contradiction)
              <;>
              (try aesop)
          exact h₆
        exact h₄
      -- Now we have h₁: ∃ (v : Vector α m) (a : α), x = Vector.push v a
      obtain ⟨v, a, rfl⟩ := h₁
      -- We need to show that push v a is in the support
      -- By the induction hypothesis, v is in the support of the sample for vectors of length m
      have h₂ : v ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) m) := by
        -- Apply the induction hypothesis
        have h₃ : ∀ (x : Vector α m), x ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) m) := by
          simpa using ih
        -- Specialize to v
        have h₄ : v ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) m) := h₃ v
        exact h₄
      -- Also, a is in the support of the sample for α
      have h₃ : a ∈ support (OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) := by
        -- Every element is in the support of the uniform sample
        have h₄ : ∀ (a : α), a ∈ support (OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) := by
          intro a
          have h₅ : a ∈ support (inst✝.selectElem) := inst✝.mem_support_selectElem a
          simp only [OracleComp.uniformSample] at h₅ ⊢
          exact h₅
        -- Specialize to a
        have h₅ : a ∈ support (OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) := h₄ a
        exact h₅
      -- Now we need to show that push v a is in the support of the sample for vectors of length m + 1
      -- The sample for vectors of length m + 1 is Vector.push <$> (sample for m) <*> (sample for α)
      -- Since v is in the support of the sample for m and a is in the support of the sample for α,
      -- push v a is in the support of Vector.push <$> (sample for m) <*> (sample for α)
      have h₄ : (Vector.push v a : Vector α (m + 1)) ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1)) := by
        -- The sample for vectors of length m + 1 is Vector.push <$> (sample for m) <*> (sample for α)
        -- Since v is in the support of the sample for m and a is in the support of the sample for α,
        -- push v a is in the support of Vector.push <$> (sample for m) <*> (sample for α)
        have h₅ : (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1)) = Vector.push <$> (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) m) <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝ := by
          rfl
        rw [h₅]
        -- Use the fact that v is in the support of the sample for m and a is in the support of the sample for α
        -- to show that push v a is in the support of Vector.push <$> (sample for m) <*> (sample for α)
        apply mem_support_map_of_mem <;> simp_all [h₂, h₃]
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      -- Therefore, push v a is in the support of the sample for vectors of length m + 1
      exact h₄
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by sorry
  probFailure_selectElem := by sorry

simp_all [Nat.recAux, Vector.support_inj, Vector内部控制]
    <;>
    (try cases x <;> simp_all [Vector.push, Function.comp]) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try use (x.pop, x.back)) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Vector.support_inj, Vector内部控制])

Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1)) <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) := by
            -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
            have h9 : y ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1)) := by
              apply ih
            have h10 : a ∈ support (OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) := by
              apply inst✝.mem_support_selectElem
            -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
            have h11 : (Vector.push y a : Vector α (m + 1 + 1)) ∈ support (Vector.push <$> (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1)) <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) := by
              -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
              apply support_map.mpr
              -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
              apply support_bind.mpr
              -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
              use y
              -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
              <;> simp_all [support_map, support_bind]
              <;>
                (try aesop) <;>
                (try simp_all [Vector.ext_iff, Vector.length]) <;>
                (try aesop) <;>
                (try norm_num) <;>
                (try simp_all [Vector.ext_iff, Vector.length]) <;>
                (try aesop)
            exact h11
          -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
          have h9 : (Vector.push y a : Vector α (m + 1 + 1)) ∈ support (Nat.recAux (pure #v[]) (fun m ih => Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1 + 1)) := by
            simp_all [Nat.recAux, Function.comp_apply]
            <;>
              (try aesop)
          -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
          simpa [ha] using h9
        -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
        exact h4
      -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
      simpa [add_assoc] using h3
  -- Use the fact that the push operation is supported on all pairs of supported elements
  exact h2
Vector.push <$> ih <*> OracleComp.uniformSample α inst✝) (m + 1))
